records.ety: [L. stimulatus, p. p. of stimulare to prick or goad on, to incite, fr. stimulus a goad. See Stimulus.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stimulated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stimulating.]

defs: To excite as if with a goad; to excite, rouse, or animate, to action or more vigorous exertion by some pungent motive or by persuasion; as, to stimulate one by the hope of reward, or by the prospect of glory.
quotes: To excite and stimulate us thereunto.

defs: To excite; to irritate; especially, to excite the activity of (a nerve or an irritable muscle), as by electricity.
